## of the
## Commonwealth of Kentucky



Passed at the Extraordinary Session of the General Assembly, which was begun in the city of Frankfort, Kentucky, on Monday, November the eighteenth, 1963, and ended Thursday, December the fifth, 1963.

FOREWORD
The 1954 General Assembly imposed upon the Legislative
Research Commission the duty of furnishing the printer correct
copy of bills and resolutions for inclusion in the Kentucky Acts .
Section 57.160 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes stipulates that
bound copies of Kentucky Acts be delivered and distributed
within sixty days after the end of a session .
In the preparation of this volume of the Kentucky Acts of the
Third Extraordinary Session of 1963 the Secretary of State has
furnished access to copy of filed enrolled bills and resolutions .
The staff of the Legislative Research Commission compared the
prepared page proofs with the enrolled bills . Mr. Courtenay J.
Walker , Administrative Assistant to the Director , has had pri-
mary responsibility for the preparation of this volume .
Frankfort , Kentucky
December 6, 1963
WILSON W. WYATT
Chairman
CHARLES WHEELER
Director

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
OF THE
COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
PASSED AT THE EXTRAORDINARY SESSION OF THE GENERAL
ASSEMBLY , WHICH WAS BEGUN IN THE CITY OF FRANKFORT ,
KENTUCKY , ON MONDAY , NOVEMBER THE EIGHTEENTH , 1963 ,
AND ENDED THURSDAY , DECEMBER THE FIFTH , 1963 .
CHAPTER 1
(H. R. 4)
A JOINT RESOLUTION making an appropriation to the Legislative
Research Commission for expenses incurred in connection with the
Third Extraordinary Session of 1963 of the General Assembly , and
declaring an emergency.
WHEREAS , additional personnel are necessary to properly expedite
the business of the Senate and the House of Representatives ; and
WHEREAS , the present appropriation of the Legislative Research Com-
mission is insufficient to provide adequate service to the General
Assembly ;
NOW , THEREFORE ,
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. That there be appropriated out of the General
Expenditure Fund , to the Legislative Sessions Fund , for the pay-
ment of salaries and other expenses of the Third Extraordinary
Session of 1963 of the General Assembly , the sum of nine thousand
dollars per calendar day during the Third Extraordinary Session
of1963 .
Section 2. That there be appropriated out of the General
Expenditure Fund , to the Legislative Research Commission , for
the purpose of providing additional services to the General As-
sembly during the Third Extraordinary Session of 1963 of the
General Assembly , the sum of one thousand seven hundred and
fifty dollars per calendar day during the Third Extraordinary
Session of 1963 .

2 ACTS , THIRD EXTRAORDINARY SESSION
Section 3. The appropriations made in Sections 1 and 2 of
this resolution shall be available for expenditure during the 1963-
1964 fiscal year .
Section 4. Whereas , the General Assembly is now in session
and it is necessary that funds be available immediately , an emer-
gency is declared to exist and this resolution shall become effec-
tive upon
itspassage and approval by the Governor
, and shall
be retroactive
toinclude payment
ofcompensation
tosuch per-
sonnel from and including November
18, 1963
.
Approved November 27
, 1963

CHAPTER 2
CHAPTER 2
(S. B. 1)
3
AN ACT relating to the Judiciary , with particular relation to the nomi-
nation , election and compensation of its members.
WHEREAS , it is in the public interest that Judges of the Court of
Appeals and Circuit Judges be nominated and elected on a non-
partisan basis ; and
WHEREAS , under Sections 112 and 133 of the Kentucky Constitution
the members of the Court of Appeals and Circuit Judges are re-
quired to be paid adequate compensation for their services , which
requirement , under the decisions in Wright v . Oates , Ky . 314 S. W.
2d 952 , Perkins v . Sims , Ky ., 350 S. W. 2d 715 , and Matthews v . Allen ,
Ky . , 360 S. W. 2d 135 , takes precedence over restrictive provisions
of Sections 235 and 246 of the Kentucky Constitution ; and
WHEREAS , the compensation of the Judges of the Court of Appeals is
grossly inadequate, being the lowest in the nation for any state court
of last resort ; and
WHEREAS , the compensation of the Judges of the Circuit Court likewise
is grossly inadequate , also being the lowest in the nation for trial
courts of general jurisdiction ; and
WHEREAS , in the light of compensation standards prevailing for other
positions in public service in this state and for judicial positions in
the Federal service the General Assembly finds that the salaries
specified in this Act conform to minimum reasonable requirements
of adequacy ; and
WHEREAS , it is in the best interests of the public that the holders of
judicial offices be paid compensation such as to command the dignity
and respect for the offices which are essential to the preservation of
our system of government ;
NOW , THEREFORE ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. ( 1 ) The names of the candidates for the offices
of Judge of the Court of Appeals and Circuit Judge shall be placed
on the voting machine at the regular election in a column or line
headed or preceded by the words "Judicial Ballot," in such a
manner that the casting of a vote for
all
ofthe candidates
ofone
party
ascontemplated
byKRS 125.050
(3) will not operate
to
cast
avote for such judicial candidates
. When voting machines
are not used
, the names
ofsuch judicial candidates shall
beplaced
upon
aseparate ballot styled
"Judicial Ballot
." Each candidate

4. ACTS , THIRD EXTRAORDINARY SESSION
shall be designated by the name of the political party by which he
was nominated , or if an independent , by the designation specified
in his nominating petition .
(2) The names of the candidates shall be arranged in such
order as may be determined by
lot
inthe same manner
asprovided
insubsections
(2)and
(3)
ofKRS 119.140
.
Section
2. The salary
ofeach Judge
ofthe Court
ofAppeals
,
payable out
ofthe State Treasury
, shall
be
$20,000 per annum
.
Section
3. The salary
ofeach Commissioner
ofthe Court
ofAppeals appointed pursuant
toKRS 21.150
,payable out
ofthe
State Treasury
, shall be
$17,500 per annum
.
Section
4. The salary
ofeach Circuit Judge
, payable out
ofthe State Treasury
, shall be
$12,500 per annum
. The com-
pensation payable under KRS 21.305 for services
as
aspecial
commissioner
ofthe Court
ofAppeals shall
be
inaddition thereto
.
Section
5. Sections 64.496 and 64.498
ofthe Kentucky
Revised Statutes are repealed
.
Section
6. Whereas
, the present compensation
ofthe
Judiciary
isgrossly inadequate and does not meet the Constitu-
tional requirements
ofadequacy
,
anemergency
isdeclared
to
exist and this Act shall become effective on January
1, 1964
, ex-
cept for Section
4which shall become effective on January
6,
1964
,
atthe time the term for Circuit Judges commences
.
Approved December
